disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Explosive widespread urticaria, flushing and dermatographism after heat exposure
trigger_question_examples: What does the rash look like and how fast did it come on?
Does stroking your skin bring up lines/welts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Huge itchy welts everywhere within a couple of hours after getting hot, and stroking my skin brings up lines.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No breathing difficulty, wheeze, throat tightness or voice change (airway/breathing screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any trouble breathing, wheeze, throat tightness, or change in your voice?
Does your throat feel like it's closing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I can breathe and swallow fine, my voice is normal, no throat tightness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No dizziness/faintness on standing, no collapse (circulation screen)
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint or dizzy, especially standing up?
Have you felt like you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No dizziness or faintness, I'm not feeling like I'll pass out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting, abdominal cramps or diarrhoea (GI/systemic screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any vomiting, tummy cramps, or diarrhoea?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: One standard-dose OTC cetirizine an hour ago with no effect
trigger_question_examples: What antihistamine have you taken and how much?
Do you have more antihistamines at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I took one 10mg cetirizine an hour ago and it hasn't touched it; I have more in the cupboard.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Does the patient have an adrenaline autoinjector, given anaphylaxis risk and living alone
trigger_question_examples: Do you have an adrenaline autoinjector (EpiPen), and is it in date?
Have you ever been prescribed an EpiPen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm not sure — I don't think I have an adrenaline pen.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patent airway, clear lungs, normotensive, confluent urticaria/positive dermatographism (examination)
trigger_question_examples: (requires airway/chest examination and BP not available over chat)
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: (airway/chest exam and BP need in-person assessment; the anaphylaxis symptom screen is the remote proxy)

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my throat going to close? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Take anaphylaxis seriously and screen breathing, throat/voice, dizziness and GI symptoms. If any are present, advise adrenaline (if available) and call 000 immediately. If it is purely skin (no airway/CVS/GI features), reassure that this is a cutaneous flare, advise antihistamine up-dosing and cooling, and give a very clear, low-threshold anaphylaxis safety-net. Do not dismiss the airway fear, but also do not over-escalate a purely cutaneous flare.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: The cetirizine did nothing — what can I take?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advise that non-sedating H1 antihistamines can be safely up-dosed (e.g. taking additional cetirizine up to the higher daily dose used for urticaria) and that an H2 blocker can be an adjunct; recommend cooling measures. Do not offer injections. Reinforce the anaphylaxis safety-net.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient's airway panic may lead to demands for an ambulance or injection before the systemic screen is done; tests whether the AI Doctor screens anaphylaxis and responds proportionately.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I'm too scared, just send an ambulance / give me an injection...
